label: 345_carbon tax_carbon_metcalf_carbon taxes
description: The United States considers using carbon taxes as part of its fiscal strategy to address large federal deficits. Economists and policymakers debate the effectiveness of such measures in generating revenue while also reducing emissions, with discussions spanning from immediate economic recovery needs to long-term fiscal sustainability.

earliest_articles.title: Analytical General Equilibrium Effects of Energy Policy on Output and Factor Prices
earliest_articles.excerpt: Analytical General Equilibrium Effects of Energy Policy on Output and Factor Prices Using an analytical general equilibrium model, we find closed form solutions for the effect of energy policy on factor prices and output prices. We calibrate the model to the US economy, and we consider a tax on carbon. By looking at…

earliest_articles.title: Web Chat: Simplifying Taxes
earliest_articles.excerpt: 1:01 William Gale: Climate change IS A TAX ISSUE. If ever there were a case for a tax on a commodity, it applies to carbon emission, which creates general costs that are not borne (exclusively) by the emitter. But we need to be careful how we go forward. We need world-wide action on this, not just US action, since…

newest_articles.title: Distributional Effects of Reducing Carbon Dioxide Emissions With a Carbon Tax: Working Paper 2021-11
newest_articles.excerpt: This paper describes CBO’s method for measuring the distributional effects of a tax on carbon emissions and the agency’s rationale for choosing that method, while also comparing it with CBO’s prior method and methods used by other researchers. Summary By Dorian Carloni and Terry Dinan Putting a price on emissions of…

central_articles.title: Carbon Taxes as Part of the Fiscal Solution
central_articles.excerpt: I. Introduction The United States faces large federal fiscal deficits in the immediate future, the next 10 years, and the longer term.
